Introduction.name: Juyeon Won
Introduction.body: I single-handedly own the development and maintenance of the security product PrivKeeper P, implementing device integrations, concurrency handling, and security features hands-on. Lately I've been extending into RAG/LLM-based automation.

About.title: About Me
About.paragraph-1: I'm a backend & security engineer who single-handedly owns the day-to-day of PrivKeeper P, an enterprise password-management security product.
About.paragraph-2: I cover everything from development and maintenance to heterogeneous device integration and security certification — a top contributor with ~265 commits across two repositories. In particular, I fixed real concurrency and stability issues (lock acquisition, duplicate-run prevention, recovery on abnormal termination) when changing passwords across many cameras at once.
About.paragraph-3: For the security-function certification, I personally implemented plaintext masking in logs/backups, TLS 1.2 enforcement, DRBG-based random generation, SHA-256, and large-scale upgrades of OpenSSL, Tomcat, JDK, Spring, and Log4j.
About.paragraph-4: I enjoy defining recurring field problems myself and solving them with diagnostic tools (OnviProbe, utilApp) and LLM-based incident-response automation.
About.paragraph-5: I work by narrowing down root causes to the end, grounded in logs and raw response payloads.

onviprobe.title: OnviProbe — ONVIF Camera Diagnostic Tool
onviprobe.description: A Go tool I built on my own initiative that predicts whether PrivKeeper can change a given camera's password — without touching the camera. It reproduces the product's ONVIF decision logic, adds an independent ONVIF-standard verdict, and categorizes failure causes into codes.

utilapp.title: utilApp — Broadcast Encryption (CAS) TS/EMM Analyzer
utilapp.description: An in-house diagnostic tool built with Qt/C++ that inspects broadcast-encryption streams in the field — currently in real internal use. Real-time TS packet analysis, scrambling (encryption) status checks, and EMM reception verification.

aiauto.title: PrivKeeper Incident-Response Automation (AI · LLM)
aiauto.description: An internal automation tool that auto-classifies customer inquiries/incidents with Gemini·GPT and drafts responses and similar-case references. Designed and built mostly single-handedly as part of an AI capability project.

more.text: I've also delivered a RAG-based CCTV anomaly-response solution, a government VLM R&D project (owning the LLM part, ongoing), and security-function certification. Most are internal/security products, so the source is private.

Contact.title: Contact Me
Contact.intro: If you'd like to collaborate on a product, or reach out about a role,
Contact.cta: you can contact me at wjy6454@naver.com or via the links below.

bullets: Single-handedly own PrivKeeper P, an enterprise password-management security product — development, maintenance, customer technical support, and security certification (~265 commits across two repos, top contributor).

Solved real concurrency/stability issues when changing passwords across many cameras at once — lock acquisition & finalize guarantees, duplicate job-code prevention, and state recovery on abnormal termination.
Built a new DB password-change feature end to end (page, logic, length constraints, change history, stability).

Hands-on security hardening — plaintext masking in logs/backups, TLS 1.2 enforcement & SVMSSDK TLS integration, DRBG random generation, SHA-256, and large-scale upgrades of OpenSSL, libcurl, and Log4j to obtain security-function certification.
Analyzed and fixed ONVIF/VMS integration failures based on logs and raw response payloads; continuously expanded supported devices (network switches, cameras, etc.).
Built diagnostic tools (OnviProbe, utilApp) and LLM-based incident-response automation to streamline repetitive work.
